def: s.f. A kind of musical instrument made of two or more gourds, and having two strings; a kind of lute (syn. bīn).
more: s. f. A sort of fiddle.
s. f. A (musical) instrument like the bīn, but having three or four pumpkins and only two steel strings ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: A form of the formative pl. of the interrog. pron. kaun (see kin, and kis).
v. a. 3d pers. sing. fem. past tense (of کرنون in Braj) Made.
= P کنکاش kinkāsh, s.f.(?) = kingāj, &c., q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī), A bondsman, slave, servant; — poor wretch; — name of a people; name of a tribe of demons.
more: n. m. 1. See dās (1, 2). 2. A poor wretch. Kinkar kī sunyo, mahārāj!
s. m. A slave, a servant.

def: = P کنکاج kinkāj, s.f.(?) = kingāj, &c., q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) = kinkinī, q.v. s.f. A small bell; — a girdle of small bells, or any tinkling ornaments (worn by women and children).
s.f. A small bell; — a girdle of small bells, or any tinkling ornaments (worn by women and children).
more: s. f. A girdle of small bells worn by women.

def: s.m. = kiṅkiṅī.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Ornamented or decorated with small bells, or tinkling ornaments; — a person so ornamented.
v.t. = kīnnā, q.v.

ety: Prk. किन(इ) = S. क्रीण(ति), rt. क्री
def: v.t. To buy, purchase ( = kinnā).
more: Sant. v. a. S. क्री to buy; Pr. kinaī he buys. See خويدنا 1.
v. a. To purchase, to buy.

ety: Gr. κάνναβις, or P. کنب kanab, arabicized
def: s.f. Hemp; flax.

s.m. (f. -ī), A mythical being with a human figure and the head of a horse, reckoned among the Gandharvas or celestial choristers; a class of demigods attached to the service of Kuvera, the god of riches.
more: n. m. Pālī. kinnara . Demigods in the service of Kuvera.
s. m. A demigod attached to the service of Kuvera, a celestial chorister or musician.

def: prop. n. = کنوج kanauj, q.v. s.m. The town and country of Canouj (Kanouj) in the North-west Provinces of Hindūstān (where Porus is supposed to have reigned. The name would appear to be sometimes synonymous with Antar-bed. Other forms of spelling are kannoj, kinnoj, nd kinnauj).
more: Name of a celebrated city of India, written by Europeans Canoge, or Kanouje, or Kinnoge.

def: s.m. The tree Butea frondosa; the blossom of this tree (which is of a beautiful red).
conj. But, however, still, nevertheless; again, moreover
subs: s.m. (lit. 'destroying every "but"'), One of the eleven Karaṇas or astronomical periods.

def: s.m. See s.v. کنتو kintu. conj. But, however, still, nevertheless; again, moreover
subs: s.m. (lit. 'destroying every "but"'), One of the eleven Karaṇas or astronomical periods.

def: s.m. A drug or seed used to produce fermentation in the manufacture of spirits from sugar, &c.; a ferment; yeast.
s.m. Buyer, purchaser (syn. ḵẖarīdār).
s.m. = kinwaiyā, q.v. s.m. Buyer, purchaser (syn. ḵẖarīdār).
s.f. A funnel.
s.m. Tar; pitch
s.m. A parrot; a parroquet; — flesh; — the country of Kaśmīr (Cashmere).
more: n. m. Pālī. kīro . A parrot ( totā ).

def: s.m. Penis.
s.m. A scream, a yell, a howl; the trumpeting of an elephant; the call to a sheep or goat (also kur, and kuṛṛ).
more: n. m. Eng. cry. S. कल to sound. 1. The trumpeting of an elephant. 2. A howl; yell. 3. ( kuṛṛ, ur ) The call to a sheep or goat.
wety: Inflection of کرنا (karnā, “to do”).

def: s.m. A worm (used in comp.)
more: (contract. of کيڙا) s. m. A worm. کڙکهايا kiṛ-khāyā, adj. 1. Wormeaten. 2. Marked with smallpox, pitted, spotted.
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Worm-eaten (syn. kirm-ḵẖẉurda); — marked with small-pox, pitted, spotted.

more: s. (dakh.) Worm: see کيڙا kīṛā. کيڙ کهايا مون kīṛ khāyā mūṅ (i. q. کهڙبڙا منہہ) Worm-eaten face, noisy or tumultuous mouth(?).
s. (dakh.) Blast (on corn, trees, &c.), canker ( Harris ).
subs: (-meṅ), Worms to breed or form (in), to have worms (in)
adj. (f. kīṛ-khāʼī), Worm-eaten; pitted (with small-pox); rough, rugged
s.m. Insects, reptiles, creeping things, vermin.

def: s.m. A worm, maggot, grub, an insect, a reptile; — canker, blast (on corn, trees, &c.), mildew, rot
more: s. m. A worm, an insect, a maggot, a reptile, a snake.
subs: (-meṅ), Worms to breed or form (in), to have worms (in)
adj. (f. kīṛ-khāʼī), Worm-eaten; pitted (with small-pox); rough, rugged
s.m. Insects, reptiles, creeping things, vermin.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀻𑀟 (kīḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit की॒ट (kīṭá).

def: s.m. prop. kīṛā, q.v.
more: n. m. S. कीट, Pālī, kīṭo . Any creeping thing; a worm; an insect; a maggot; grub; reptile; vermin. kīṛā dabānā , v. a. ( Slang ) To satisfy one's desire. kīṛā kultulānā, yā uchhalnā , v. n. lit . to crawl (insects in the vulva or anus ). To feel the sexual passion ( Prurigo ). kīṛā lagnā , v. n. To be worm-eaten. Sārī kītāb ko kīṛā lag gayā. kīṛe paṛnā , v. n. To have worms (in a fruit, sore, etc). Tere mūṛ meṅ kīṛe paṛyo, hāṛ hāṛ gal jāve! Khūṅṭe bāṅdh piyā ko mere jaise tū tarsāve . Play. May worms get in your brain, your bones just rot away As you my love afflict, bound 'neath your magic's sway. kiṛ-khāyā, kiṛ-khāī , adj. Pitted with small-pox.

ety: for A. قراءة, inf. n. of قرأ 'to read'
def: s.f. Reading (esp. of the Qorʼān); a proper manner of reading the Qorʼān, a correct pronunciation.

ety: P. kirj, kirij, kurć, or karać; prob. S. कृत्त
def: s.m. A segment, a slice, a small piece, bit, particle, grain
subs: or kar-ḍālnā, v.t. To cut to pieces.

ety: inf. n. of قارف qārafa, 'he went in' (to his wife
def: s.m. Having to do (with one's wife); (a woman's) having to do (with a man); — staining oneself (with vice).
adj. = kīṛahā, q.v.
adj. = kīṛahā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Containing worms, wormy, worm-eaten, weevil-eaten; blighted, blasted.
more: adj. Wormy, wormeaten. See کيرها kīrahā.

def: s.m. A parrot ( = kīr, q.v.). s.m. Tar; pitch
more: s. m. A parrot, a paroquet.

def: s.m. A Buddha; — a kind of tree.
adj. & s.m. Noble, generous; eminent; mild, benign; — the noble, &c.
more: s. plur. (of کريم) Noble, generous, eminent. ¶ kurām, 1. adj. Great, high in rank or authority, venerable. 2. s. m. Magnitude, dignity, authority. ¶ kurrām, adj. Most noble, most generous. کرام کاتبين kirāmuṉ kātibīn, Recording angels.

def: n. m. The recording angels.

ety: inf. n. of قارن qārana, 'it was in conjunction'; iii of قرن 'to be in a transition state,' &c.
def: s.m. Conjunction (of planets); propinquity, contiguity; — a period of 10, 20, 30, 40, 80, or 120 years; — an auspicious conjuncture, or season
more: n. m. قرن joining. 1. Conjunction of the planets. 2. An auspicious conjuncture or season. qirān-us-sādain . The conjunction of the two auspicious planets, Jupiter and Venus. sāhib-i-qirān , n. m. 1. One born under an auspicious conjunction of two or more planets. 2. A title of honor. sāhib-qirān , adj. Fortunate; prosperous.

(from قرن) s. m. 1. Conjunction of the planets. 2. Propinquity, contiguity. قران کرنا ḳirān karnā, To accomplish something wonderful, to perform wonders. قران السعدين ḳirānu-s-saﻌdain, The conjunction of the two fortunate planets Jupiter and Venus. ¶ ḳurʼān (from قرأ) s. m. The book containing the precepts, &c. of Muḥammad (Coran or Alcoran). قران اٿهـانا ḳurʼān uṭhānā, To swear. قران پرهاتهہ دهرنا ḳurʼān par hāth dharnā, To swear. قران خوان ḳurʼān-ḵẖẉān, A reader of the Coran; a person deposed from rule or dignity. قران کا جامه پـﮩنا ḳurʼān kā jāma pahannā, To swear excessively.
subs: s.f. The conjunction of the two fortunate planets Jupiter and Venus
To accomplish something wonderful, to perform wonders

def: s.f. (but m. in S. and sometimes in H.), A ray (of light), beam; sunbeam, moonbeam; — a tassel (of gold or silver)
more: n. f. S. किरण 1. (Sant. rāṅt ) A ray; beam. 2. Gold or silver tassels.

s. f. Ray, beam (of light), sunbeam, moonbeam. ¶ karaṇ ( कर्ण ) The hypothenuse of a triangle, or a diameter or diagonal of a square, parallelogram, &c. a secant. ¶ karṇ or karan ( कर्ण ) s. m. The ear; the helm of a boat, &c. کرن پهول karṇ-phūl, s. m. A kind of ear-ring. کرن مول कर्णमूल ( कर्ण Ear, and मूल Root) karṇ-mūl, A parotis, or swelling near the ear. ¶ karaṇ ( करण ) s. m. Making, acting, doing, to make, to do; cause, instrument.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit किरण (kiraṇa). First attested in c. 1518 as Middle Hindi کرن (krn /⁠kiran⁠/).

def: s.m. 'What is sold'; grocery, spices, condiment.
more: n. m. S. क्रयण or क्रव्य Spices.
v. a. S. कृ cast. To sift by turning round in a winnowing fan (different from phaṭaknā , q. v.).

ety: kir˚ = S. किर(ति), rt. कॄ+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To sift or separate by turning round in a winnowing fan (cf. phaṭaknā, from which it differs); — to adjust.
s.f. A superior description of goods' cart (made of wood instead of bamboos); a coach.
more: n. f. S. क्रेयाञ्ची A superior description of goods' cart, made of wood instead of bamboos.

def: s.m. A writer, clerk, an English copyist in a public office (generally of mixed European and Native descent); a convert to Christianity
more: n. m. Cor. of clerk. 1. An English clerk in a public office. 2. A convert to Christianity. kirānī urdū , n. f. The Hindustānī language as spoken by Eurasians or Europeans; unidiomatic Urdu. kirānī-khānah , E. n. m. An office.
subs: s.f. The Hindūstānī language as spoken by Eurasians or Europeans, unidiomatic Urdū

def: n. S. कराओ A small pea; Pisum arvense . (?).
more: n. m. 1. The marriage of a widow with the brother (generally with the younger brother) of the deceased husband. The custom prevails among the Jāṭs, Gūjars, Ahīrs , and other inferior tribes, etc. as it did amongst the Jews. Biyāh na karāo, jhūṭ mūṭ kā chāo . Rus. Prov. Nor wife nor mistress he will make, but love-making all for love's sake. 2. Concubinage. karāo k. , v. a. 1. To marry a widow. 2. To keep a mistress. 3. To keep a man (to live with).

def: s.m. (dialec.), A merchant, shop-keeper; a corn-chandler (cf. kirānā).
s.f. = قراءت qirāʼat, q.v. s.f. Reading (esp. of the Qorʼān); a proper manner of reading the Qorʼān, a correct pronunciation.
s.m. The weight of a grain of the carob-tree (or the seed of the Abrus precatorius), a carat, the twenty-fourth part of an ounce, a weight of four barley-corns.
more: (from قرط To divide into small parts. A seed of the Abrus precatorius, taken out of the pod, κερατιον , is the weight in question) s. m. A carat, the twenty-fourth part of an ounce: four barley-corns: Alf.

def: s.m. (f. -ī, or -in, or -inī), Name of a degraded mountain tribe, a tribe of mountaineers who live by the chase (the Kirrhadæ of Arrian); a man of that tribe; a savage.
more: s. m. A savage, a tribe of mountaineers who live by the chase.

def: n. f. Good elocution, esp. of the Qurān. gair qābil-i-qirat . G. G. Illegible.
more: (from قرأ Heb. To read) s. f. Pronunciation, reading.

ety: P. karv, kirau; and cf. kirnā
def: adj. & s.m. Decaved, broken, tattered; rugged, jagged; — a decayed or hollow tooth; — endive, bitter lettuce.
more: 1. s. m. A hollow tooth. 2. adj. Broken, rugged, tattered. 3. Endive.
adj. Ten millions. کروڙپي karoṛ-patī, Possessor of a karoṛ of rupees. کروڙکهکهہ karoṛ-khukh, s. m. A liar. (Pers. plur. کروڙها).
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit कुरु (kuru).

ety: S. कृमि+dim. aff. nā = lā = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. (rustic) A worm.
s.m. A small pea, Pisum arvense.
more: n. m. A hollow or worn-out tooth.
s. m. A small pea. (Pisum arvense).

def: s.m. Hire, fare, rent, freight
more: n. m. Hire ( bhāṛā ). Roz phir ātī hai launḍī merī jā-kar khālī, Bhāṛ meṅ jāë kirāyā! voh kareṅ ghar khālī . Wom. Jān Sāhab. My slave girl empty-handed comes back every day, Fire burn the rent! let him just leave my house I say. kirāyā ughānā , v. a. To collect the rents, etc. kirāë chalānā, yā denā , v. a. To let; hire out.

s. m. Hire, fare, rent. کرايه چلانا kirāya chalānā, To let (any thing the use of which is in moving, as a horse, cart, &c.). کرايه دار kirāya-dār, A tenant, renter, hirer. کرايه کرنا kirāya karnā or کراٴي کرنا kirāʼe karnā, To let, to hire. کرايه لينا kirāya lenā, To hire, to rent.
subs: (or kirāʼe) ćalānā or kirāʼe denā (-ko), To let, to hire out (a horse, or a cart, and the like)
vulg. kirāye-dār, s.m. A tenant, renter, hirer
s.m. One who lets on hire
(or kirāʼe) karnā, v.t. To hire, rent; to let
(or kirāʼe) lenā, v.t. To take on hire, &c., to hire, rent; — to realize the rent or fare (of, -kā; from, -se, a person)

def: n. A tenant ( bhaṛaitī ). kirāë-dār utarnā , v. n. 1. To occupy a house (the tenant). 2. ( Slang ) To be pregnant. kirāya-zamīn . Ground-rent. kirāë k., yā lenā , v. a. To rent; hire. kirā/?/ kī gāṛī ; (Europeans, ṭhekā-gāṛī ). A ticca; a hackney coach; a conveyance let on hire. kirāyā lenā , v. a. To realise the rent or fare. kirāya-nāmah , n. m. A lease of a house ( sarkhat , 1). kirāya-i-vāpisī ; H. phirtā bhāṛā, phirāū , n. m. Return fare or hire.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کِرَایَه دَار (kirāya dār).

def: s.f. A sword (esp. a straight one for thrusting with); a splinter.
more: n. f. S. कृत to cut. A sword.
n. m. S. किञ्चित A bit; grain ( rezah ). kirche kirche k. , v. a. To cut to pieces.
s. f. 1. A splinter. 2. (perhaps from Malay کريس krīs ) A sword (especially, a straight one to thrust with).

ety: P. kirj, kirij, kurć, or karać; prob. S. कृत्त
def: s.m. A segment, a slice, a small piece, bit, particle, grain
subs: or kar-ḍālnā, v.t. To cut to pieces.

ety: kird = kard, fr. kardan, rt. Zend kar = S. कर(कृ)+aff. dār = tār = Zend tar = S. तार
def: s.m. Doing, action, act, deed; work, business, labour, employment, profession, trade, art, occupation; manner, conduct; way, practice, habit, wont.
more: n. m. 1. Act; deed. 2. See bān , 2, 3.
s. m. Action, doing, labour, deed, art, business, manner, conduct.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کِرْدَار (kirdār). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi کردار (krdar).

ety: for kird, see s.v. kirdār; — gār = Zend kāra = S. कार
def: s.m. The Creator, God the omnipotent (syn. kartār, q.v.).

ety: dim. of kīṛā; and = S. कीट+इका
def: s.f. A small worm or insect; an ant; a leech (syn. joṅk)
subs: s.f. A woman who applies leeches.

def: s.f. prop. kiṛī, q.v.
more: n. f. 1. An ant (چيونتَي). 2. (Wom.) A leech ( joṅk ). kīṛī-vālī , n. f. A woman who applies leeches. kīṛiyāṅ lagānā , Wom. v. a. To apply leeches ( joṅkeṅ lagānā ).

def: s.m. Dung, filth; — a person who does not cleanse himself from filth, or does not take care to avoid pollution.
more: s. Dung, filth; a person who cleanses not himself from filth, or takes not care to avoid pollution.

def: s. Dung ( Gilchrist ).
s. (dakh.) Dung, muck ( Harris ).

s.m. Wink, nod, glance; looking languishingly through half-shut eyes, amorous look or gesture, side-glance, ogling, blandishment, coquetry (cf. kaṭāksha); — a phenomenon, a wonder, a miracle; a talisman, a charm.
more: n. m. 1. A phenomenon; a miracle. 2. A talisman; charm. 3. Ogling; an amorous look or gesture; blandishments. karishma dikhānā , v. n. 1. To perform a miracle. 2. To work wonders.
s. m. Ogling, an amorous look or gesture, blandishment, a wink, a glance.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کرشمه (kirišma), probably from Ancient Greek χᾰ́ρῐσμᾰ (khắrĭsmă, “grace, favour, gift”), from χᾰρῐ́ζομαι (khărĭ́zomai, “I show favor”), from χᾰ́ρῐς (khắrĭs, “grace”), from χαίρω (khaírō, “I am happy”).

def: s.m. A tiara, diadem, crest, a wreath or any ornament used as a crown.
more: s. m. A diadem, a crest.

def: s.f. Virtue.
more: s. f. Fame, renown. کيرت وان kīrat-wān ( कीर्तिमान् ) adj. Renowned. ¶ kīrit (perhaps for कृत ) Virtue.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Decorated with a diadem, &c., crested, crowned; — one who is adorned with a diadem; a king; an epithet of Arjuna.
v.n. See s.v. kirnā. v.n. To decay, moulder, crumble, wear out, break off; fall off; to be turned, become notched or jagged (the edge of a knife, &c.); to be or feel ashamed (of, -se), be shy (of)
n. f. Grit; sand. 1. Arī! is nāj māṅ kirkal saë, dāhoṅ chhaṛ pichhoṛ kar pīsyo! Rus. Wom. I say, this grain is full of grit. Pound and sift it well before you grind!
s.f. A kind of ornament, a pendant to a nose-ring.
more: n. f. A pendant to a nose ring.

ety: prob. fr. S. किटकिटाय; but cf. कर्कर
def: s.f. Grittiness; the sound of anything gritty under the teeth; — grating, creaking (of a door, &c.)
subs: v.n. To sound gritty (under the teeth, as bread, &c.); to creak, grate.

def: (dakh.) To creak ( Harris ).
adj. (f. -ī), Gritty, sandy; — (fig.) spoiled, marred
more: adj. S. कर्कर 1. Gritty; sandy. Jījī! turat kī rahāī oṛ chākī to chūn kirkirā hī ho saë . Rus. Sister! the mill just drest, the flour turned out is full of sand. 2. Spoilt; marred. kirkirā k. , v. a. 1. To make gritty. 2. To spoil; mar. Sārā mazā kirkirā kar dīyā. kirkirāhaṭ, kirkirāpan , n. f. Grittiness. izzat kirkirī k. , v. a. To disgrace one; to drag one's honor through the mire.
subs: v.t. To make gritty; to spoil, mar, &c.

def: s.f. = kirkir, and karkarāhaṭ, qq.v. s.f. Grittiness; the sound of anything gritty under the teeth; — grating, creaking (of a door, &c.)
subs: v.n. To sound gritty (under the teeth, as bread, &c.); to creak, grate.

def: v.n. To sound gritty, &c. ( = kirkir karnā, q.v.s.v. kirkir); to crackle ( = karkarānā).
more: v. n. 1. To be gritty. 2. To creak; grate.
v. n. To gnash one's teeth with rage.
v. n. To be gritty, to grate.

def: v.t. To grind or gnash (the teeth).
s.m. Grittiness ( = kirkir).
s.f. Grittiness, sandiness, &c. ( = kirkir); — a creaking or rickety piece of furniture; lumber
subs: s.m. A receptacle for old and worn-out furniture, or lumber, or odds and ends.

def: s.f. A mote, or particle of dust fallen into the eye.
more: s. f. A mote, or particle of dust fallen into the eye.

def: n. f. A mote or particle of dust fallen into the eye.

ety: Pehl. kereym, kraêm; Zend kerema; S. कृमि
def: s.m. A worm
more: n. m. S. कृमि Pālī, kimī . A worm, esp. Ascaris vermicularis. kirm-pīlā , n. m. The silkworm. kirm-khurdah ; H. kīṛā-khāyā , adj. Worm-eaten. kirm-i-shab-tāb , n. m. A fire fly; glowworm.
s. m. A worm. کرم پيله kirm-i-pela, s. m. The silkworm. کرم خورده kirm-ḵẖụrda, adj. Wormeaten.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَرَم (karam), from Arabic كَرَم.

ety: kirm, q.v.+dim. aff. ak = S. क
def: s.m. A small worm, vermicule; a firefly.
more: s. m. 1. The fire fly. 2. (dimin. of کرم) A small worm, vermicule.

r: kirmānī, kirm, ānī, jismānī, kirmān, ī
ety: kirm, q.v.+A. aff. ānī; cf. jismānī; — or kirmān, pl. of kirm+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. Wormy; worm-like
s. m. Vermicelli. ( Gilch. ).
s.f. The palās-tree.

ety: P. also qirmiz, for kirmiz = S. कृमिज kr̤imi-ja, 'worm-engendered'
def: s.m. Kermes; crimson; cochineal.
more: s. m. Crimson, cochineal. (Hence, Chermes or Kermes).
wety: PIE word *kʷŕ̥mis Borrowed from Classical Persian قرمز (qirmiz), from Arabic قِرْمِز (qirmiz), from Persian *کرمست (*kermest), ultimately from Proto-Indo-Iranian *kŕ̥miš (“worm”).

def: adj. Crimson-coloured, crimson, scarlet; — s.m. Crimson colour or dye; — s.f. Scarlet cloth.

def: s.m. The Hindu god-man Kr̤ishṇ.
more: n. prop. The most celebrated form of Vish̤ṇu or rather Vish̤ṇu himself; being distinct from the ten avatārs or incarnations. He was the younger brother of the third Rāma (or Balarāma ). His parents were Vasudeva and Devakī; but he was brought up in the house of Nanda and Yasodā, to conceal him from the tyrant Kansa, to whom it had been predicted that a child, the eighth of this family, would destroy him. In the family of Nanda he passed his youth, among the gopas and gopīs. During his childhood he vanquished the serpent Kāliya, and slew many giants and monsters. Afterward, he put the tyrant Kansa to death, protected Yudhish̤ṭhira, and kindled the war described in the Mahābhārata. He is the Apollo of the Hindūs, and is supposed by Colonel Wilford to have lived about 1300 years before Christ.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit कृष्ण (kṛṣṇa). Doublet of کرشن (kirśn) and کنھیا (kanhaiyā).

Mīr kā hijr meṅ visāl huā, E lo! qissa hī infisāl huā . Mīr. Mīr absent in the flesh his God hath found, Behold our differences now closed and bound! 2. Dekho ghar kā qissā bāhar na jāë! qissah pāk k. , v. a. 1. To settle a dispute. Yā to pās-i-dosti tujh ko but-i-be-bāk ho, Yā mujhī ko maut ā jāë kī qissa pāk ho . Zauq. Be true to me, dare-devil darling, through life! Or death be my saviour and cut short the strife! 2. To discharge a debt. De dilā kar qissa pāk karo . 3. To kill, murder. qissah-khuāṅ, dāstān-go , n. m. A story-teller or reciter. qissah-khuānī , n. f. Story-telling. qissa k., uṭhānā, yā khaṛā k. , v. a. To quarrel; make a quarrel. Pahlī sakhī yoṅ kahe, sakhī rī! merā piyā bhaṅg pīyā kare, Pī-…

(from قصّ) s. m. 1. A tale, a story, narration. (Plur. قصص ḳiṣaṣ ). 2. ( met. in Hind.) A quarrel, a dispute. قصه خوان ḳiṣṣa-ḵẖẉān, A storyteller. قصه کوتاه ḳiṣṣa kotāh, In short, in one word. قصه کوتاه کرنا ḳiṣṣa kotāh karnā, To settle a dispute. قصه مختصر ḳiṣṣa muḵẖtaṣar, In short.
subs: v.n. To raise a commotion or dispute, get up a quarrel
(-kā), To put an end to a quarrel, to settle a dispute; to put an end to (a person, &c.), to wipe out, to kill
(-kā), A dispute to be settled; a subject of contention or quarrel to be put an end to
s.m. A story-teller, a reciter of tales
adv. The short of the story (is that); in short, in a word, briefly
To shorten the story, to cut the matter short; to settle or put an end to a dispute
adv. In short, &c. ( = qiṣṣa kotāh, q.v.)
(-kā), (One's) story to be cut short; to come to an end, to die.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic قِصَّة (qiṣṣa).

def: s.m. A Christian priest, a presbyter, a bishop.
more: s. m. A presbyter, a chief of the religious, a bishop.

ety: inf. n. of قسط 'to appoint what is just,' &c.
def: s.f. Portion; instalment, dividend; a stipulated rate and time of payment (of revenue, &c.); — a tax
more: n. f. Instalment; partition; dividend ( bāṅṭ , 2). qist bāṅdhnā , v. a. To pay by instalments. qist-bandī , n. f. Paying the revenue by instalments; the revenue demand roll paid in instalments. qist-kār , n. m. A payer of a debt or tax by instalments. qist munqazī , G. G. An instalment in arrear. qist-vār , adv. By instalments.
s. f. 1. Portion. 2. Instalment. 3. Tax. قسطبندي ḳist̤-band-ī, s. f. Settlement of the revenues or taxes by instalment.
subs: (-kī), To arrange to pay (a debt, &c.) by instalments; to pay by instalments
s.f. Settling for payment (of the revenue, or taxes, or a debt) by instalments; payment (of the revenue, &c.) by instalments; an obligation for the discharge (of a debt, &c.) by instalments
s.m. One who pays (revenue, or a debt, &c.) by instalments
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قِسْط (qist), borrowed from Arabic قِسْط (qisṭ).

def: adv. See s.v. kis. the formative of the oblique cases of the sing. of the interrog. pron. kaun, 'who,' &c.
subs: or kis-t̤aur, In what manner? how? — kis-t̤araf, In what direction? whither? — kis-qadr, To what degree? to what extent? how much? — kis-kis, Which? (of several); with what various; with how many (used interrogatively)
or kis-wāst̤e, or kis-kāje, On what account? from what motive, or reason, or cause, or occasion? why? wherefore? — kis-waqt, At what time? when?

def: s.f. corr. of kishtī, q.v.
(prob. the format. of the old H. pron. koʼu = koʼī), the old form of kisī, the formative of koʼī; see kisī.
adv. Why? &c. (see s.v. kis); — for the reason that, because ( = is-wāst̤e ki).
For what reason? why? wherefore?

s.f. = kisbat, q.v. s.f. The case (or bag) in which barbers, surgeons, &c. keep their tools; — a piece of leather worn by a bhishtī or water-carrier on his left hip, on which he rests the mashk or water-bag
subs: s.m. A book containing the histories of barbers, surgeons, &c.; the bhishtīs or water-carriers also have such a book.

ety: for A. کسوة, fr. kasw, inf. n. of کسو 'to clothe'
def: s.f. Dress, apparel, robe, habit; appearance; (mel.) figure, form; manner.
more: n. f. 1. A barber's or surgeon's case. 2. The piece of leather worn by a bhishtī or waterman on his left hip, on which he rests his masak or water bag.
(from کسا Heb. To cover, to clothe) s. f. 1. Dress, appearance, robe, habit. 2. ( met. ) Figure, form, manner.

def: s.m.(f. ?), Sediment, dregs, dirt; dregs of oil (in a lamp); dregs collected in a huqqā-snake.
more: s. m. 1. An insect, worm. 2. The dregs of oil in a lamp, or that collected in a ḥuḳḳa snake.

def: s.m. A worm, maggot, insect, reptile (see kīṛā).
adj. & adv. (dialec.) = kittā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), How much? what quantity? — kitte, how many (see kitnā).
more: Sant. adv. S. कुत्र Where; whither. Chhān binā ab kit maiṅ baiṭhūṅ ? Rus. No cover o'er my head, where can I sit?

def: s.m. Cutting; a section, &c.

ety: for A. قطعة qit̤ʻat, n. of un. fr. قطع; see qat̤ʻ
def: s.m. A cutting, segment, section, division; a fragment, piece, scrap; a detached portion; a patch or plot (of ground); — a strophe; — distichs corresponding in measure and rhyme, but without a mat̤laʻ
more: n. m. 1. A detached portion; scrap; fragment; piece. Āpkā ek qatٴa khat āyā . 2. A patch or plot of ground; a tract. 3. A kind of verse, in which the ever. lines rhyme and the meaning of the first verse of each stanza is completed in the last. qataī, qatan , adv. Completely; finally; absolutely; positively; peremptorily; once for all.

s. m. 1. A section, a cutting, a fragment, a division. 2. A strophe. قطعه بند ḳit̤ʼaﻌ-band, adj. A kind of verse, in which the meaning of the first verse of each stanza is completed in the last. ¶ ḳat̤ʼﻌ or ḳut̤ʼaﻌ, A division, plot, parcel or portion of land, segment, portion; cut, shape.
subs: s.m. A kind of verse (consisting of pairs of baits or couplets) in which the meaning of the first bait is incomplete without the bait immediately following (or in which the meaning of the first verse of each stanza is completed in the last).

ety: imperf. part. of kīnā = karnā, qq.v.
def: part. Doing, making ( = kartā).
s.f. A book; a writing, despatches; scripture; a letter
more: n. m. pl. kutab , کتب wrote. A book kitāb-i-ilahī, kitāb-i-āsmānī, kalām majīd , n. m. The Quran. kitāb pe chaṛhānā , v. a. To register; put down; make a note of.

(from کتب Heb. To write) s. f. A book, writing, despatches, scripture, a letter. کتاب الهي kitāb-i-ilāhī, The holy book, holy writ. کتابخانه kitāb-ḵẖāna, A library. کتاب رو kitāb-rū, adj. Oval or long-faced. کتاب فروش kitāb-farosh, A bookseller.
subs: or kitāb-ě-āsmāni, or kitāb-ě-majīd, The holy book, holy writ, the Qorʼān
(-ko), To enter in a book, to book, register, put down, make a note of
s.m. (f. -ī), One who has a revealed Book, i.e. a Jew, a Christian, or a Moḥammadan (syn. ahl-ě-kitāb); — a vendor or a maker of books.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کِتَاب (kitāb), borrowed from Arabic كِتَاب (kitāb). Displaced Old Hindi कतेब (kateba), borrowed from the same term. Compare Punjabi کتاب (kitāb), Sindhi ڪِتابُ (kitābu).

ety: for A. کتابة kitābat, inf. n. of کتب 'to write'
def: s.f. The profession of a copyist.
(for A. کتبة kitabat, pl. of kitābat), s.m. pl. Writings; inscriptions; epitaphs, &c.
more: s. m. plur. (of کتابت) Inscriptions, writings.

ety: for A. کتابة kitābat, inf. n. of کتب 'to write'
def: s.f. The profession of a copyist.
more: n. f. The profession of a copyist. kitāb-rū, yā kitābī chehrah , n. m. A long face.

def: adj. Of a book, relating to a book or writing; in possession of a revealed Book; — like a book, oblong; — s.m. One who is (or a people who are) in possession of a revealed Book, i.e. a Jew, a Christian, or a Muslim
more: adj. Relating to a book or writing. The phrase is applied, by Mahomedans, to Jews and Christians, as in possession of a revelation or scriptural authority from the same Deity, although, according to some of them, corrupted or superseded. kitābī ādmī , A bookish man. kitābī-ilm , n. m. Book-learning or knowledge.
adj. Of a book, like a book oblong. کتابي چـہره kitāb-ī chihra, A long or oval face.
subs: s.m. A long or oval face

def: s.f. (prov.) A small book.
more: (dimin. of کتاب) s. f. A small book.

def: (for ke-taʼīṅ, qq.v.), postpn. To, up to; for; on, at (often used as equivalent to ko, the postpn. of the dative and acc. cases).
more: (or dakh. kitaiṅ or kataiṅ for کي تئين) postpos. To, up to, for, on, at, or denoting the objective case.

def: adj. (prov.) = kitek, and kittā, qq.v. adj. (f. -ī), How much? what quantity? — kitte, how many (see kitnā).
more: n. m. S. कटक, Pr. kadaa . A large army.
adj. (dakh.) Some, how much? see کتنا kitnā.

def: To suffer distress, to twist or twine, to be very uneasy.

ety: inf. n. of قاتل qātala, 'to fight with,' iii of قتل 'to kill'
def: s.f. Fighting, battle, slaughter.
more: (from قتل) s. f. Battle, slaughter, fighting. ¶ ḳattāl, adj. Great slayer, killer, murderer.

def: s.m. A guitar.
s.f. A string (of camels, &c.); a string, line, row, series, order, rank, range, train, &c.
more: n. f. قطر dropping of water, etc., tying camels in a line. See panktī . Row; string; range; train; procession; file; rank and file ( silsilah , 1). Ūṅṭoṅ kī qatār. qatār bāṅdhnā , v. a. To string together; place, set, or form in order; arrange in a series; range; marshal. qatār meṅ , adv. In a line; in succession ( silsilevār ).
To bind, to tie together; which meaning is preserved in II. conj. of قطر) s. f. A line, string (of animals), rank, order, series, row, range, file.
subs: (-kī), To string together; to put or place in a line or row, or in order; to arrange in a series; to marshal
adv. In a line, or series; in succession.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic قِطَار (qiṭār).

def: adj. & adv. (rustic) = kittā; and kaʼī-ek, qq.v. adj. (f. -ī), How much? what quantity? — kitte, how many (see kitnā).
s.f. Fame, &c. (see kīrti).
(dakh.) Tick (small noise). کٿ کٿ کرنا kaṭ-kaṭ karnā (dakh.) To click ( Harris ).

v. a. 1. To gnash or grind (the teeth). 2. To urge cattle to go faster; to cry gee wo. Arai! ṭuk ek baldān ne kiṭkiṭā jo dinghāveṅ! Rus. I say! stir up your cattle a little and make them step out! kiṭkiṭ'ī , n. f. Grinding one teeth.
s.m. Concealing; concealment.
s.m. The thin pellicle on a date-stone; — name of the dog which accompanied the seven sleepers
more: s. m. 1. The thin pellicle on a date stone. 2. Name of the dog which accompanied the seven sleepers. نقيروقطمير naḳīr o ḳit̤mīr, adv. Minutely, exactly, to the point.
subs: adj. & adv. Minute, particular, exact; — minutely, exactly, to the point.

ety: Prk. केत्तिलओ; S. कियतिकः; see kittā
def: adj. & adv. (f. -ī), How much? what quantity? (kitne) how many? — as many as; several; some
more: adv. S. कति or कियत Pr. keddahā, kettia . 1. ( kuttān ) How many; what number; how much. 2. As many as; several. 1. Baṛe baṛe bahe jāëṅ, gadhā kahe kitnā pānī? Prov. Great ones away swept down a heap, the donkey asks 'is the water deep?' (A fool would dare where better men ill fare). 2. Chuṛyāṅ chubkī-dār haiṅ, chahiyeṅ tumheṅ kitek, Yahāṅ ān-ke pahan jā adhik ek se ek . Play. Chaubolā. Lac-bangles shining ho! as many as you want, Come try them—one and all they're superexcellent. kitnā hī . However; howsoever; howmuchsoever.
How much? how many? some, several: see کتا kittā. کتني ايک kitne ek, Some, some few. کتناهي kitnā-hī, Howsoever, howsoever much or many.
subs: adv. Howsoever, however much or many, how-much-soever.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀬 (kittiya) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀦- (-na-), from Sanskrit *कियत्त (*kiyatta), variant of कियत् (kíyat). Doublet of کِتَّا (kittā). Cognate with Punjabi ਕਿਤਨਾ /کِتْنا (kitnā).

def: s.m. A kind of sour dish.
adj. (f. -ī), How much? what quantity? — kitte, how many (see kitnā).
more: n. f. Bloodshed kaṭā-kaṭī . A general slaughter or massacre. kaṭai'yā ; A. qātil , n. m. One who makes a general massacre. adh-kaṭā , adj. Half-cut.

adj. 1. Hard; strong; robust; able-bodied. Haṭtā kaṭṭā hai . 2. Mortal; deadly. Kaṭṭā bairī . 3. Strict; orthodox; bigoted. Kaṭṭā musalmān. kaṭṭā ; Sant. se ; Tir. pitthā , n. m. A large fat louse. Paṛe sīs māṅ seroṅ kaṭṭe, phūhaṛ samjhe chaṭṭe baṭṭe . Rus. Fat lice full many seers' weight on her head, between the finger nails crack crack they're dead, the ninny hears but rattle sounds instead.
How many? how much? as many, some.
ex: میرَا کُتَّا ہَمیشَہ بِلِّی پَر بَھوںکْتَا ہَےْ۔
merā kuttā hameśa billī par bha͠uktā hai.
My dog always barks at a cat.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (kutta).

def: s.m. Secretion; excretion; excrement; dirt, filth, impurity
s.f. (old H.), corr. of kīrtti, q.v. s.f. Mention, report; good report, praise; fame, renown, glory; — a proper name
subs: To make a name, to acquire renown
(f. -matī), or kīrtti-vān, or kīrtti-vant (f. -vatī), adj. & s.m. Praised, celebrated; famous, renowned, glorious; — a famous person, &c.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit कति (kati), ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *kʷóti.

def: s.f. = kiwāṅć, q.v. s.f. Cowach, Mucuna prurita; or Dolichos pruriens.

ety: v.n. fr. قوم; see qawām, and qiyām
def: s.m. That on which anything rests, or in which it consists; substratum; essence, substance; competency; consistency; syrup; materials, ingredients, requisites.
more: n. m. 1. See chāshnī , 1. 2. Materials; ingredients; requisites.

def: adj. Syrup-like, syrupy; thick, substantial, &c.
s.f. Cowach, Mucuna prurita; or Dolichos pruriens.
s. Name of a plant, cowitch. (Dolichos pruriens).
s.m. (dialec.), See kiwāṛ.
more: n. m. S. कपाट Pr. kapaṭo . 1. A leaf of a door. Ek pahelī maiṅ kahūṅ—uṛī jāt hai Lankā, Ādhā nām kivāṛ hai, aur ādhā nām hai pankhā . Charade. Ans. Paṭ-bījnā . A glow-worm. A riddle hear—far off Ceylon its flight can span, Its first half is a door, its second is a fan. 2. A door. kivāṛ band k., bheṛnā, yā denā , v. a. To shut a door. kivāṛ'ī , n. f. 1. Dim of kivāṛ , q. v. 2. See pardah , 4. 3. ( chhātī ke kivāṛ ). The intercostal bones. (?)

ety: Prk. कवाडो and कवाडओ; S. कपाटः and कपाटकः
def: s.m. A door; leaf or panel of a door; shutter or fold of a door; gate (of a city), entrance.
s.m. (dialec.) = kiwāṛā, q.v.

ety: Prk. कवाडो and कवाडओ; S. कपाटः and कपाटकः
def: s.m. A door; leaf or panel of a door; shutter or fold of a door; gate (of a city), entrance.
s.f. A small door, a small gate; a wicket; — the piece which covers the chest in an angarkhā, q.v.; — an intercostal bone (?).

ety: perf. part. of karnā; and = Ap. Prk. किया and कइओ, Prk. करितओ, or कलिट्ओ = S. कर+इत+कः
def: part. (f. kiʼī, and kī), Done, performed, &c. (see karnā); — s.m. Action, deed; doings.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀺𑀁 (kiṃ), 𑀓𑀺 (ki), from Sanskrit किम् (kim, “what, which”). Related to کِہ (ki). Compare Nepali क्या (kyā).

def: (from قوف) s. m. Appearance, countenance, mien, likeness, air, manner, mode, representation.

ety: for A. قيافة qiyāfat; prob. a post-classical form fr. قوف 'to follow in the footsteps' (of
def: s.m. Appearance, semblance, likeness, resemblance; imitation, representation; countenance, mien, face, look; air, port; manner, mode; physiognomy (syn. firāsat)
more: n. m. قوف following the foot teps. Face; look (صورت 2). Qayāfe se zāhir sarāpā shaūr, Jabiṅ par barastā shujā at kā nūr . Mir Hasan. qayāfa-shinās , n. m. A physiognomist. qayāfa-shināsī , n. f. Physiognomy ( ilm-i-qayāfah ).

def: s.m. Standing upright; rising up; up-rising, insurrection; resurrection; standing position (in prayer); standing still; stagnancy; stationariness, rest; congealed state (of water, &c.); stability, permanence; stay, halt; settlement, residence
more: n. m. قوم stood. 1. Standing position in prayer. 2. Stay ( ṭairāo , 2). 3. Existence; being. 4. Durability; permanence ( sthirtāī , 1). Qayām-i-jism-i-khākī hai nafas par, Havā par hai binā apne makāṅ kī . Arshad. This earthy frame with breath must go as't came. This mausion hath no firmer base than air. qayām-i-aman , n. f. Preservation of the peace. qayām-pizīr , adj. Stationary; at rest; reposing. qayām k. , v. a. To stay; lodge ( ṭhairnā , 3—5). Āp kab tak qayām kareṅge?
(from قوم) s. m. 1. Standing erect, resurrection. 2. Stability, permanence. 3. Attention. 4. Residence, settlement.
s. m. Standing position in prayer.
subs: being; appointment (to a charge, &c.); looking after, keeping, preservation (of the peace, &c.); application, attention (to)
adj. Stationary, at rest; stable, permanent, &c.
(-meṅ), To stay, remain (at), to settle, reside, lodge; to make a halt, to halt.

ety: for A. قيامة, fr. qiyām, q.v.
def: s.f. The resurrection, the last day; — confusion, commotion, tumult, uproar, extraordinary to-do; anything extraordinary; a scene of trouble or distress; a great calamity; excess; — adj. & adv. Wonderful; excessive, very great; heavy, grievous, oppressive; — wonderfully; excessively, extremely, very
more: n. f. 1. The general resurrection; the last day. Qayāmat ke din tumhārā dāman hogā, aur merā hāth! Wom. On the judgment day my hand upon your skirt will be. (Our account will be settled then). 2. ( Met .) Calamity ( āfat , 1). 3. Weeping and wailing. 4. Commotion; tumult; uproar ( gul shor ). qayāmat , adj. 1. Very great (عاقبت 2). Tum to qayāmat jhūṭ bolte ho . Wom. 2. Heavy; oppressive ( bhārī , 12, 15, 16). Woh din hijr kā us pe shāmat huā, Use kāṭnā din qayāmat huā . Mīr Hasan. qayāmat uṭhānā, barpā k., yā machānā , v. n. To make a hell of a noise or row. qayāmat ānā , v. n. 1. To come (the resurrection). 2. To befal (a great calamity). Woh jab pahlū se uṭhte haiṅ qayāmat ā hī jātī hai . Shaidā. qayāmat k., yā toṛnā . 1. To bear hard upon. 2. To oppress.
s. f. 1. The general resurrection, the last day. 2. ( met. ) Calamity, excess. 3. Excessively great, excessive, extremely. قيامت کرنا ḳiyāmat karnā, 1. To oppress. 2. To do any thing wonderful.
subs: or qiyāmat bar-pā karnā, or qiyāmat maćānā, v.n. To raise a commotion, &c.; to make a terrible to-do, &c.
(-par), To let troubles, &c. like those of the resurrection loose (upon), to bring calamity (upon); to practise great oppression (upon), to oppress grievously
(-par), The resurrection to break (upon); a great calamity to befall
v.n. To raise a tumult, &c. ( = qiyāmat uṭhāna); — to practise oppression (upon, -par), to oppress ( = qiyāmat toṛnā); — to do something wonderful, to achieve wonders.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قِیَامَت (qiyāmat), from Arabic قِيَامَة (qiyāma).

def: s.f. Stability; steadiness.

s.f. Bed (of a garden or field); a border; a frame; a bed (of salt, e.g. non-kī kyārī); — a field or plot of ground under cultivation having a raised border, bank, or hedge round it; a garden with a fence or border round it.
more: n. f. S. केदार 1. A bed (of a garden or field). Kyārī kyoṅ na banvāī, ḍomnī kyoṅ na gavāī? Du-sukhnah. Ans. Bel na thī . Why was n't the field prepared, why did n't the ḍomnī sing? Ans. 1. I had n't a hoe. 2. I hadn't the money. 2. A frame. 3. A salt bed. Non kī kyārī .
s. f. 1. A bed (of a garden). 2. A frame.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑁂𑀆𑀭 (keāra), from Sanskrit केदार (kēdāra, “field”).

ety: inf. n. of قيس; and also inf. n. of iii of قيس 'to measure, to compare (with
def: s.m. Measuring (by or with); comparing (with); measurement, comparison; — reasoning, ratiocination; a syllogism; — regular form, analogy, rule; judgment, opinion; thought, conception; fancy; theory; supposition, conjecture, guess
more: n. m. قيس measuring, comparing See bichār , 3, 6, aṭkal, andāz , 4, and andāzāh , 1. 1. Thought; judgment; opinion. 2. Conjecture.
(from قيس) s. m. Measuring or comparing (by reason), judgment, opinion, supposition, thought, guess, theory, a syllogism. قياس کرنا ḳiyās karnā, To guess, think, suppose.
subs: v.t. To think, judge, opine, conceive, imagine, suppose, guess; to estimate

def: adv. By analogy; by conjecture or guess, conjecturally.
more: adv. By guess ( aṭkal se ). qayās se bāhar . Beyond conception or calculation. qayās k., yā lagānā , v. a. To guess ( aṭkalnā , 1). qayās meṅ ānā , v. n. To be conceivable.

ety: for A. کياسة, inf. n. of کيس 'to be quick,' &c.
def: s.f. Quickness of parts; vivacity; ingenuity, sagacity; shrewdness; cunning

def: n. m. S. कोद्रव A small grain; Paspalum kora , Wat. kodoṅ dalānā , v. a. 1. To cause one to grind kodoṅ. Chāhe kodoṅ dalā le, chāhe manḍvā pisāle . E. Prov. I'll grind for you, I'll pound for you, kodoṅ or manḍvā , whatsoe'er it be. 2. To set one a hard task; to make one undergo hard labor. kodoṅ de-ke paṛhnā , v. n. 1. To get a cheap and inferior education. 2. To fail to pay the extra two pence for manners. Kodoṅ hī de-ke paṛhe ho? Prov. Saying. kodoṅ kā bhāt , An insignificant thing. Kodoṅ kā bhāt kin bhātan meṅ, mamiyāṅ sās kin sāsan meṅ? Prov. What's kodo among different kinds of rice, of what account is the wife's uncle's wife among mothers-in-law? marne par kodoṅ darnā , Bhoj. v. n. To oppress; treat cruelly.

In a moment; speedily, ( abhī , 2). koī sā, koī sī , adj. Certain; some. koī koī , Rare; scarce; a few. koī na koī . Somebody or other; some one or other; any. koī hai . Who waits? koī-haī , n. A soubriquet of officers of the Bengal Presidency. na koī , Nobody; no one; none. Na-koī ātā thā ghar meṅ, na-koī jātā thā, Na-koī god meṅ le-kar mujhe sulātā thā . Nobody came to the house, nobody from it went, Nobody in his lap fed me, nor me to sleep e'er sent. ☞ During the absence of her husband who was abroad a stranger was a frequent visitor of the wife's. The woman's child once asked his mother who this man was. She said in reply ' Na-koī ' no body, and na-koī henceforward was to the child the stranger's name. When the husband, on …

more: n. f. S. कोकिल, , Pr. koëlo . 1. The black or Indian cuckoo; Cuculus Indicus . 1. Bā/?/ṅ bolā kāg, dāëṅ bolī koyaliyā, bhale bhale sugan bhaë . Wom. song. A crow cawed on the left, the cuckoo's note heard on the right—an omen excellent. 2. Tū kyā bole, kārī ri koyaliyā, chhūṭo bābul ko des rī . Wom. song. s. f. 1. Name of a bird. (Cuculus). 2. A flower. (Clitoria ternatea).

def: s.f. = kokilā or koʼil, q.v.; — s.m. = koʼilā, 'charcoal.' s.m. (lit. 'Kokil-eyed'), The plants Asteracantha longifolia, or Capparis spinosa, Barleria longifolia, Ruellia longifolia
subs: adj. Kokil-voiced, speaking like the kokil, eloquent as the kokil, sweet-voiced.

def: s.m. Charcoal; — live coal, firebrand
more: n. m. Charcoal; coal. Khusro! aisī prīt kar jūṅ Hindū kī joë, Patī prīt ke kārne jal bal koëlā hoë . Your love be, Khusro, like the Hindu widow's flame, Who burned for her lord's love, till charcoal she became. koële kī kān . A coal mine.

def: s.f. = koʼī, q.v. s.f. The water-lily, Nymphæa lotus; N. pubescens (cf. koṅʼī).

ety: also g̠ūć; prob. = gushn, Zend varshni = S. वृष्णि
def: s.m. A ram (esp. a fighting ram).

s.m. (lit. 'Who is awake?'), The day of full moon in the month Asin (ko-jāgar is said to have been the call of Lakshmī, who, descending on this night, promised wealth to all who were awake; hence the night is spent in festivity and games of chance in honour of this goddess).
s.m. A cuckoo ( = kokil); the ruddy goose, Anas casarea; a wolf; a frog; — scientia modorum diversorum coeundi, a quodam Kok Pandit explicata, unde nomen
more: n. f. Basting; stitching. koknā, kok denā , v. a. To baste; stitch.
n. m. 1. ( kok shāstr ) Scientia modorum diversorum coeundi, aquodam Kok Pandit explicata, unde nomen . 2. The ruddy sheldrake; Anascasarca ( surkhāb ).
s. m. 1. Scientia modorum diversorum coeundi, a quodam Kok Pandit explicata; unde nomen. 2. Name of a bird, the ruddy goose. (Anas casarca).
subs: s.m. Name of an indecent treatise on the art of love; describing the merits and excellencies of the sexes, &c. (ascribed to a Pandit named Kok; some, however, assign the formation to the observation of the loves of the kok or ruddy geese).

def: s.f. Long stitches in sewing, basting.

s.m. = کوکه koka, q.v.
more: s. m. 1. A fosterbrother, nurseʼs child. 2. A small nail, a tack. 3. A kind of lotus.

def: s.m. A bud, an unblown flower; the red lotus, or red water-lily ( = S. kokanada); the style or spike which shoots up from the plantain (containing the flower); — a small thorn, a prickle; a small nail, a tack
subs: s.f. The red lotus, or water-lily.

def: s.m. A foster-brother, nurse's child.
more: Mah. n. m. A foster brother or sister.
